items.quote: those decisions come from CFO and CEO
only 1 buyer-side contact role logged on this opportunity

items.area: Confirmation of Daniel's internal review outcome

metrics.narrative: Daniel Tzegay described real operational pain but no quantified metric. On the 1 May 2026 discovery call he said the invoice approval process involves 'too many too many approval process' back-and-forth by email, and referenced wanting Workday-style timestamped audit trails for visibility. No dollar figure, headcount, or cycle-time number was quoted, so the business case remains qualitative.

champion.narrative: Daniel showed real, sustained engagement across a ~37-minute call — asking detailed follow-up questions and stating the platform 'will streamline a lot of our services' — but he is not a confirmed champion. He has no proven access to the CFO/CEO who own the decision, and there is no evidence since 1 May 2026 that he took the material to his team/director as he said he would; the 20 May 2026 rep follow-up has gone unanswered as of 9 Jul 2026.

competition.narrative: Stratasys runs Oracle ERP (Fusion/NetSuite variants) for financials and operations, already uses Microsoft Copilot for some contract-related automation, and runs CLM through a 'Mailbox' platform under a newly-signed 10-year term (out of scope here). The real alternative Zycus is displacing for this deal's eProcurement/eInvoicing scope is the status quo: manual, email-based invoice approval. No dedicated S2P/P2P competitor has been named.

identify_pain.narrative: Daniel confirmed specific, first-hand pain: a slow, email-driven invoice approval cycle between AP and internal business users ('there's a lot of time consuming back and forth of emails, and I'd rather just have a streamline of one service'), plus a desire for better supplier risk visibility given government-contract exposure. This is buyer-articulated and specific, but it hasn't been prioritized or escalated beyond Daniel's own function.

paper_process.narrative: The deal has not progressed to any contracting discussion; Stratasys is still at Qualified with a single completed discovery call. No paper-process artifacts (MSA, SOW, pricing) have been introduced. Notably, Stratasys just signed a 10-year contract on its 'Mailbox' CLM platform, which locks contract-lifecycle-management out of scope for this deal for the foreseeable future.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has been identified or engaged. Daniel stated explicitly on the 1 May 2026 call, 'those decisions come from CFO and CEO. I have to present this out to them and see what their thoughts are before we connect again' — meaning Zycus has zero confirmed access to the actual decision-maker, and there is no evidence the CFO or CEO have seen any Zycus material.

decision_process.narrative: The process beyond Daniel is unmapped. He confirmed he can only 'make recommendations' and that the CFO and CEO make the final call, and said he would align with his team and director before deciding next steps — but no timeline, committee, or approval sequence has been shared since.

decision_criteria.narrative: Daniel surfaced several real evaluation angles on the 1 May 2026 call: a single system of record to replace email-based invoice approvals, timestamped/audited workflow modeled on his prior Workday experience, robust supplier risk screening (cyber, financial, litigation) given Stratasys' government-contract exposure, multi-language support, and interest in autonomous negotiation for tail-spend purchases. None of this has been formalized into a written requirements list or RFP.

items.name: Stage: Initial Interest → Qualified
items.summary: Opportunity advanced to Qualified, but this appears procedural given the parallel health alerts showing cold engagement and no decision maker — worth confirming what actually justified the stage move.

items.name: Opportunity health review — evaluation process clarity
items.summary: Internal flag to get clarity on the buyer's evaluation stages and find ways to influence the process, suggesting we currently lack visibility into how Stratasys will actually make its decision.

items.name: Opportunity health review — org chart mapping
items.summary: Internal flag to build an org chart and map key contacts, indicating our contact coverage at Stratasys is currently too thin to navigate the account.

items.name: Opportunity health review — APD approval
items.summary: Internal flag to review and approve the Account Plan Document, a housekeeping step to keep the account plan current.

items.name: Opportunity health review — low inbound communication
items.summary: System flag noting inbound communication from the prospect has been low, reinforcing that Stratasys has gone quiet on their end.

items.name: Opportunity health review — decision maker not identified
items.summary: Internal flag confirming we still haven't pinned down who the actual decision maker is at Stratasys, a key gap given the stage advancement.

items.name: Opportunity health review — multi-threading
items.summary: Internal reminder to keep engagement multi-threaded rather than relying on a single contact, likely prompted by the thin org chart.

items.name: Opportunity health review — identify more contact roles
items.summary: Internal flag to identify additional contact roles within Stratasys, consistent with the broader theme of insufficient account mapping.

items.name: Opportunity health review — prospect gone cold
items.summary: System alert that there's been no inbound communication from Stratasys in 14 days, the clearest signal that buyer engagement has stalled.

items.name: Outbound follow-up reminder
items.summary: System-generated nudge noting 10 days have passed without an outbound email, prompting the rep to send a follow-up to re-engage the account.

day_summary.overall: Stratasys was bumped from Initial Interest to Qualified, but the health-check flags tell a more cautious story than the stage change suggests: no inbound from the prospect in two weeks, no outbound email in ten days, and the decision maker still isn't identified. This looks like an internal process/scoring update rather than genuine buyer momentum — the deal is stalled on engagement and needs to be re-threaded before it can be called truly qualified.

cro_panel.intro: One genuinely deep discovery call establishes real interest and a well-articulated pain point, but the deal has since gone quiet for nearly seven weeks with no economic buyer engaged and only one confirmed contact; a cooling, early-stage, single-threaded deal

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

critical_signals.text: The one substantive call so far (1 May 2026) went deep and positive, with the buyer driving detailed questions on invoicing automation and supplier risk, but he committed to reconnect in a couple of weeks and has gone quiet for roughly seven weeks since.

There is no live rival vendor in the fight; the real competitor is the status quo manual email-based invoice approval process, and contract-lifecycle management is permanently out of scope because the buyer just signed a 10-year deal on their existing CLM platform.

The buyer specifically wants a single, timestamped, auditable approval workflow (modeled on a prior Workday experience) rather than the current email back-and-forth, a concrete and winnable requirement if re-engagement happens.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is cooling: 69 days since the last verified Salesforce activity (1 May 2026), and the one confirmed rep follow-up since (20 May 2026) has no logged buyer reply.

north_star_verdict.math: Today 2026-07-07; close_date 2026-09-01 = 56 days out. Opportunity created 2026-05-01 (same day as the sole substantive buyer call), so the deal has spent all ~67 days of its life in Qualified with no reported advance to Formal Evaluation. LastActivityDate is 2026-05-01 (67 days ago); Next_Step__c logs a rep follow-up email on 2026-05-20 (48 days ago) with no reply on record since. At this pace, and with the economic buyer (CFO/CEO) never engaged, the deal cannot realistically reach Shortlisted/Vendor Selected before the 1 Sep 2026 close date without an immediate re-engagement.
evidence: Only one substantive buyer call on record (1 May 2026); no further engagement logged since.
Rep follow-up email sent 20 May 2026 (Next_Step__c) has no logged reply after roughly seven weeks.
Daniel Tzegay stated the decision rests with the CFO and CEO, neither of whom has been contacted or named.
Close date is 56 days out while the deal sits at Qualified stage with no economic buyer engagement.
north_star_verdict.headline: Single-threaded Qualified deal, cooling for 67 days since its only real discovery call; the 1 Sep 2026 close date is not achievable without an immediate re-engagement and access to the CFO/CEO who hold the decision.

flags: Multi-thread beyond Daniel Tzegay: get his director and the Israel-based sourcing lead into a call before the deal advances past Qualified.
Build the ROI story on invoice-approval and supplier-risk time savings now, so Daniel has numbers to carry into his CFO/CEO pitch.
Re-engage on a different channel (phone, not just email) given the 20 May follow-up went unanswered for seven weeks; the deal is cooling.
Build a path to the economic buyer (CFO/CEO) early, since Daniel has stated he can only recommend, not decide.
best_practice_check.summary: The two highest-impact levers right now are breaking single-threading and re-opening the conversation directly, since email has stalled for seven weeks.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer-voiced preference for Zycus over any named alternative has been captured; interest was expressed in specific capabilities (AP Smartdesk, supplier risk, Merlin Intake) but not framed as a vendor preference (Avoma call 2026-05-01).

competitive_position.summary: No dedicated procurement suite competitor is actively in the fight. The real alternative being displaced is the status quo manual, email-based invoice approval process; Microsoft Copilot and the Stratasys 'Mailbox' CLM platform came up as adjacent tools but CLM is out of scope given a newly-signed 10-year term.

competitors.name: Status quo / manual email-based invoice approval
competitors.quote: There's just too many too many approval process. Right? If we could do it where everything is timestamped...
competitors.how_we_win: AP Smartdesk / eInvoicing directly addresses the named pain of slow, email-driven approvals with a single auditable, timestamped workflow.

competitors.name: Microsoft Copilot / Mailbox (CLM, out of scope)
competitors.quote: For contract, we'll use Mailbox system... that is something new that we just signed up for 10 years, so I don't see us moving away from that... something that we have implemented last year or so is Copilot because Copilot does that for us also.
competitors.how_we_win: Not applicable for CLM (locked for 10 years); Zycus positioned Merlin Intake as a complementary front-door layer rather than a CLM replacement.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: One genuinely deep discovery call establishes real interest and a well-articulated pain point, but the deal has since gone quiet for nearly seven weeks with no economic buyer engaged and only one confirmed contact — a cooling, early-stage, single-threaded deal.

deal_risk.text: Close date is not credible against the current state — CloseDate is 1 Sep 2026 (54 days out) while the deal is still single-threaded at Qualified with no economic buyer engaged and no reply in 7 weeks; the date should be treated as provisional until Daniel re-engages.

CLM is locked out for 10 years — Daniel confirmed Stratasys just signed a 10-year contract with their 'Mailbox' CLM platform, so no contract-lifecycle-management upsell is available in this deal for the foreseeable future.

win_position.text: Real buyer engagement secured — Daniel Tzegay spent roughly 37 minutes on the 1 May 2026 discovery call asking detailed, technical questions across eProcurement, eInvoicing/AP Smartdesk, supplier risk and Merlin Intake, and called the pitch 'something that will streamline a lot of our services.'

No economic buyer engaged — Daniel told Zycus directly on 1 May 2026 that 'those decisions come from CFO and CEO,' and neither has been named, contacted, or shown any confirmed exposure to Zycus materials.

Single-threaded — Daniel Tzegay is the only confirmed opportunity contact; a 30 Apr 2026 internal Zycus call discussed trying the Israel-based head of sourcing as a backup route, but there's no evidence that outreach happened or landed.

Buyer's own review clock has lapsed — Daniel committed on 1 May 2026 to loop in his team and director and reconnect 'in a couple weeks' (roughly by 15 May 2026); it is now 9 Jul 2026, about seven weeks past that commitment, with no confirmed reply to the 20 May 2026 follow-up email logged in Next Step.

deal_momentum.text: Cooling for 69 days — last verified Salesforce activity is 1 May 2026 (69 days ago as of 9 Jul 2026), and the one confirmed follow-up touch since (a 20 May 2026 rep email) has drawn no logged buyer reply.
No forward next step — Next_Step__c still reads 'JRA 5/20: Email to Daniel to discuss next steps with his team,' a rep-side send with no dated forward milestone or confirmed buyer response.

The one real session was deep, not a courtesy call — the 1 May 2026 discovery ran ~37 minutes with Daniel driving follow-up questions on supplier risk, invoice matching, and multilingual support, evidence of genuine (if since-quiet) interest.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment made, not yet honored — Daniel said he'd present the Zycus deck to his team/director and come back with CFO/CEO's thoughts within a couple of weeks of 1 May 2026; that has not happened as of 9 Jul 2026.
Zycus delivered its side — the rep promised on the 1 May 2026 call to 'put us all on an email thread with these slides,' and that follow-up was sent the same day.

value.name: Status quo / manual email-based invoice approval
value.quote: There's just too many too many approval process. Right? If we could do it where everything is timestamped...
value.how_we_win: AP Smartdesk / eInvoicing directly addresses the named pain of slow, email-driven approvals with a single auditable, timestamped workflow.

value.name: Status quo / manual email-based invoice approval
value.quote: there's just too many too many approval process
value.how_we_win: Land the AP Smartdesk story on the concrete pain Daniel already named (email back-and-forth, no audit trail) to displace the manual process.

packets.subject: Status quo / manual email-based invoice approval

value.name: Microsoft Copilot / Mailbox (CLM, out of scope)
value.quote: For contract, we'll use Mailbox system... that is something new that we just signed up for 10 years, so I don't see us moving away from that... something that we have implemented last year or so is Copilot because Copilot does that for us also.
value.how_we_win: Not applicable for CLM (locked for 10 years); Zycus positioned Merlin Intake as a complementary front-door layer rather than a CLM replacement.

value.name: Microsoft Copilot / Mailbox (CLM, out of scope)
value.quote: we just signed up for for 10 years, so I don't see us moving away from that
value.how_we_win: Do not contest CLM; position Merlin Intake and AP Smartdesk as complementary to the existing Copilot/Mailbox stack, not a replacement.

packets.subject: Microsoft Copilot / Mailbox (CLM, out of scope)
